title: Music system dispose() doesn't disconnect master gain - memory leak
description: In engine/music.js, dispose() disconnects individual oscillator gains but doesn't disconnect the _masterGain node from the destination. This leaves the master gain connected to the audio output even after disposal. If init() is called again, a new master gain is created while the old one leaks.

title: Use PlayerCharacter mesh instead of box placeholder
description: main.js line 62 creates createBoxMesh() as the player visual, but there's a full PlayerCharacter class in player/character-mesh.js with proper body parts, cape, and sword. The box player looks terrible - should wire up the actual character mesh.

title: Replace colossus AI moveToward with adapter-based steering
description:
Consolidate the 3 duplicated `moveToward` functions (behavior.js, titan.js, wraith.js) into a single physics-aware steering function using the adapter.

**Current state:** Each colossus has its own `moveToward` that directly mutates `position` — bypassing physics entirely.

**New approach:**
- Create `colossus/steering.js` with `moveToward(body, target, speed, dt, adapter)`
- Use `adapter.setVelocity` or `adapter.applyForce` to move the kinematic body toward the target
- Keep the clamped-step pattern to prevent overshoot: compute desired velocity, let physics handle it
- Delete the local `moveToward` copies from titan.js and wraith.js — use the shared one
- 2D version for ground colossi (sentinel, titan), 3D version for flying colossi (wraith)

**TDD:** Test that steering produces correct velocity vectors, respects speed limits, handles zero-distance gracefully, and works for both 2D/3D modes. Mock the adapter.

**Files:** colossus/steering.js (new), colossus/behavior.js, colossus/titan.js, colossus/wraith.js

title: Climbable surface patches in local space, never transformed to world space
description:
All colossus surface patches (sentinel.js, wraith.js, titan.js) are generated in local body coordinates. There is no utility to rotate/translate them to world space based on the colossus position and rotation. This means:
- The climbing system operates on stale positions after any colossus movement
- Grab detection uses local coordinates instead of world coordinates
- Surface-relative movement is calculated incorrectly when colossus is rotated

This is a fundamental issue that affects all colossus climbing.

title: Audio nodes never garbage collected - potential memory leak
description: game/main.js: playProceduralSound creates oscillators/sources/filters/gains every call. cleanupSounds only tracks metadata, not actual Web Audio nodes. Memory pressure on extended sessions.

title: Add ACESFilmic tone mapping
description: The renderer has no toneMapping set. Adding THREE.ACESFilmicToneMapping with exposure ~1.2 would instantly give cinematic color response and make the golden fog/sunlight pop dramatically. One line change in renderer.js.

title: Stamina drains while climbing even without moving
description: In player/stamina.js updateStamina (line ~74), drainStamina is called while climbing even when the player has no movement input. The player loses stamina just by being attached to a surface without moving. This contradicts the GDD which says stamina drains from climbing (implying movement). The player should be able to grip without moving and conserve stamina.

title: No loading indicator - black screen while CDN modules load
description: index.html has no loading indicator. If the CDN is slow (as in jam conditions on 4G) or modules take time to load via es-module-shims, the user sees a black screen with no feedback. The GDD requires load in under 3 seconds on 4G. Without a loading indicator, users may think the game is broken and leave before it loads.

title: Add dat.gui for runtime configuration
description: Add dat.gui (using ESM from CDN) to allow configuring different settings at runtime for debugging and tweaking game parameters.

title: isClimbing state inconsistency within single frame
description: In game/main.js lines ~598-614, isClimbing captures the OLD climbing state at line 598. Then climbing is updated at line 603. But line 614 calls isPlayerClimbing(climbing) which returns the NEW state. The prevClimbing detection at line 606 uses the OLD value but the rest of the frame uses the NEW value. This inconsistency can cause one-frame glitches where audio, particles, or camera don't properly respond to climbing state changes.

title: Fix lint warnings in world/voxel-indicators.js

title: Wire colossus meshes as kinematic bodies with collision events
description:
Add cannon-es kinematic bodies for each colossus that sync position/rotation from the AI behavior each frame, and emit collision events for combat.

**Per colossus type (sentinel, wraith, titan):**
1. Create kinematic body matching the colossus bounding shape (approximate: capsule for sentinel, elongated box for wraith, sphere for titan shell)
2. Each frame: `adapter.setPosition(colossusBody, aiState.position)` + set rotation
3. Register collision events between player body and colossus bodies

**Combat integration:**
- Player weapon hits: use `adapter.raycast` from player position in attack direction, check if it hits a colossus body or a specific part
- Colossus attacks: collision event fires when colossus body contacts player — apply knockback force via `adapter.applyImpulse`

**Wind/shockwave forces:**
- `colossus/wraith.js getWraithWindForce`: apply as `adapter.applyForce` on player body
- `colossus/titan.js getShockwaveForce`: same

**TDD:** Test colossus body creation, position sync, and collision event registration with mock adapter. Test raycast hit detection for combat.

**Files:** colossus/sentinel.js, colossus/wraith.js, colossus/titan.js, player/combat.js, game/main.js

title: Weak point indicator spheres stay at spawn position, never follow colossus
description:
In colossus/weak-point-visuals.js line ~24, impl.position.set() is called once at creation time. As the colossus moves and rotates, the glowing indicator spheres stay at their original spawn position - completely detached from the colossus body. Players see floating glowing orbs while the colossus walks away.

Also: Flash color lerp is broken - material.color = {r,g,b} doesn't work with Three.js Color objects. Must use material.color.setRGB(r/255, g/255, b/255).

title: Death animation uses sentinel-only part sets, broken for wraith and titan
description:
In colossus/death.js lines 17-24, LEG_PARTS only contains sentinel leg IDs (front_left_upper, etc.). Wraith parts (neck, chest, tail_*) and Titan parts (shell_main, left_claw_*, left_leg_*) don't match. Non-leg, non-head parts get generic treatment (slight Y drop), making death animations look wrong for Wraith and Titan.

Also: COLLAPSE phase lerps to hardcoded absolute Y positions (1.0, 0.5, 1.5) regardless of part height. A wraith neck at Y=18 collapsing to Y=1 goes underground.

title: Voxel Build System Integration: Update build.js for voxel assets and zero-build compat
description:
## Problem
Current build.js bundles all JS into a single HTML file. Voxel system adds new modules that need to be included in the build, and the zero-build CDN constraint may need updating.

## Requirements
1. Update build.js import graph to include all new voxel modules in topological sort.
2. Ensure no new CDN dependencies are needed (voxel engine is pure JS, built on existing Three.js).
3. Test that production polyshadow.html includes all voxel code correctly.
4. Verify game still loads < 3s on 4G after voxel migration (may be challenging with more complex meshing).
5. Consider lazy chunk loading to keep initial bundle small.

## Affected files
- Modified: build.js
- Depends on: all P0 voxel issues

title: Player character mesh not connected to physics position
description: In game/main.js line 91-93, PlayerCharacter is instantiated and createCharacterMesh() adds the mesh to the scene. However, the mesh position is never synced to player.state.position. The mesh stays at its initial position (0,0,0) while the internal state tracks a different position. Player appears invisible or stuck at origin while game state thinks they're elsewhere. Need to add: playerMesh.setPosition(player.state.position.x, player.state.position.y, player.state.position.z) in the update loop.

title: [bug] No tutorial or onboarding - player has no idea what to do
description:
## Description
Player spawns in the arena with no explanation of controls or objectives. There are no hints about:
- How to move (WASD/gamepad)
- How to jump (Space)
- How to grab onto colossus (E)
- How to attack (left click)
- What the objective is (kill colossi)

## Expected
At minimum, show a brief control hint overlay on first play, or show hints when approaching a colossus for the first time.

## Notes
Issue found during manual playtesting.

title: Improve stepping stone materials
description: Stepping stones are simple gray cylinders (flatShading: true). Adding moss/grass vertex colors, subtle normal variation, or moss patches would make them more interesting and integrated with the world.

title: Wind push on Wraith helps player UP instead of being a hazard
description: In colossus/wraith.js line ~429, the wind push calculation includes '+ strength*0.3' which adds upward force. This means the wraith's wind hazard actually helps the player reach the wraith rather than pushing them away or knocking them off. The wind should push horizontally/downward to be a meaningful threat.

title: Release grab gives no velocity - no jump-dismount from colossus
description: player/climbing.js releaseGrab just stops and lets gravity take over. No jump-dismount or momentum. Player drops straight down from colossus, making it hard to reach intended landing spots. Should have directional leap like Shadow of the Colossus.

description:
Remove 2 unused imports from math.js:

- `lerp` (line 2)

title: Add volumetric god ray effect
description: Sun rays through the fog layers would add dramatic atmosphere. Three.js has god rays examples that could be adapted, or a simple screen-space shader pass could simulate volumetric light.

title: Two disconnected wind current systems
description: world/wind.js has simple cylinder check, world/wind_currents.js has path-based system. Completely disconnected. Player could be in wind current per one system but not other. Path system generates lazily every frame (no caching) - significant perf cost.

title: Tide Titan head weak point always active, ignores stunned requirement
description: GDD specifies that the Tide Titan head weak point should 'only be exposed when stunned'. However, in colossus/titan.js buildCombatWeakPoints (line ~310), all weak points including the head are created with isActive: true unconditionally. The stunned check is missing entirely.

title: Physics not integrated - cannon-es created but not used for movement/collision
description: In game/main.js lines 126-135, a cannon-es physics world and player body are created. However, the player movement in updatePlayer() still uses manual velocity integration (player/movement.js) instead of reading from the physics body. The physics step is called (line 965) but its results are never read back - player.position is never updated from physicsAdapter.getPosition(). Player cannot move or interact with the world because physics collision is bypassed.

title: Player has full air control - no gravity-based jump arc
description: player/movement.js applies horizontal velocity regardless of grounded state. Player can steer mid-air exactly as on ground. No momentum-based jumping - jumps feel floaty. 180 degree direction change mid-jump is possible.

title: Voxel Migration: Main loop integration and system wiring
description:
## Problem
game/main.js (~1489 lines) orchestrates all systems. The voxel migration touches nearly every system init and update call. This issue tracks the integration work of wiring all voxel systems into the main loop.

## Requirements
1. Initialize voxel storage and chunk manager at startup (replacing island mesh creation).
2. Generate voxel islands (hub + 3 arenas) via voxel island generator.
3. Create voxel colossi meshes instead of procedural geometry.
4. Create voxel player character.
5. Update per-frame loop: chunk loading/unloading, dirty mesh processing.
6. Replace island ground height queries with voxel queries.
7. Replace direction indicators positioning for voxel world.
8. Update ending sequence (island convergence) for voxel islands — may need to move chunks rather than meshes.
9. Ensure arena transitions work with voxel terrain (teleport to voxel island rather than heightmap island).

## Affected files
- Modified: game/main.js (massive changes)
- Depends on: ALL other voxel issues (this is the final integration)

title: No stun-from-damage mechanic - wraith and titan cannot be stunned
description: GDD: 'Certain actions or environmental elements can stun a colossus, exposing weak points'. Sentinel has basic stun, but Wraith has no internal STUNNED transition. No stun-from-damage mechanic exists anywhere. Wraith CLIMBING_BACK state is unclimbable and unattackable.

title: Two disconnected wind current systems
description: world/wind.js has a simple cylinder check (isInWindCurrent) while world/wind_currents.js has a full path-based system. These are completely disconnected - the player could be in a wind current according to one system but not the other. Additionally, wind_currents.js has mutable/immutable API inconsistency: addCurrent/removeCurrent create new objects while update/getForceAt modify in-place. The path-based system also generates wind paths lazily every frame (no caching), causing significant perf cost.

title: Missing favicon causes 404 error on every page load
description: The browser requests /favicon.ico which doesn't exist, producing a 404 error in the console. While not a gameplay issue, it's unprofessional and the error clutters the console during debugging. Add a simple inline SVG favicon.

title: Voxel Particles and Effects: Adapt combat feedback, particles, and death effects
description:
## Problem
Current effects use traditional Three.js primitives: Sprites for damage numbers, SphereGeometry for hit flashes, Points for dust/ember particles. Must adapt for voxel context.

## Requirements
1. **Particle system**: Current dust/ember particles (world/particles.js, Three.js Points) should work as-is since they're not terrain. May want voxel-shaped particles for consistency (small cube sprites instead of points).
2. **Combat feedback** (engine/combat-feedback.js):
   - Damage numbers: Sprites work fine, no change needed.
   - Hit flash spheres: Consider voxel-sized cube flashes or expanding voxel bursts.
   - Screen shake: No change needed (camera-based).
3. **Weak point destruction**: Current system darkens + scales down spheres. For voxels: destroy/remove the glowing voxels when weak point is destroyed, replace with dark/cracked blocks.
4. **Death animation** (colossus/death.js, colossus/death-integration.js): Currently applies per-part transforms (position/rotation/opacity). For voxel colossi: scatter individual voxels or dissolve voxel parts with gravity.
5. **Colossus health opacity**: Current applyHealthOpacity() sets material transparency. For voxels: progressively remove or darken voxels as health decreases.

## Affected files
- Modified: engine/combat-feedback.js, colossus/weak-point-visuals.js, colossus/health-visual.js, colossus/death.js, colossus/death-integration.js, world/particles.js
- Depends on: polyshadow-8bq (voxel colossi), polyshadow-ehu (voxel materials)

title: Generate procedural normal maps for terrain and colossi
description: All surfaces look flat despite being 3D geometry. Generating noise-based normal map textures on canvas and applying to materials would make terrain and colossi look like textured rock instead of colored plastic. Even simple noise-based normals would dramatically improve quality.

title: Wire up post-processing bloom pipeline
description: The post-processing system in engine/post-processing.js computes bloom intensity/threshold every frame and shouldEnableBloom() returns true during combat, but no EffectComposer is wired up. Adding UnrealBloomPass from Three.js addons would make runes, embers, and the wraith glow actually bloom. This is the single biggest visual improvement with minimal code.

title: Touch control overlay is non-functional stub
description:
engine/touch-overlay.js only exports createTouchLayout which returns coordinate positions. There is no rendering code, no event handling, and no touch-to-action mapping. Mobile users see no touch controls at all. Additionally, the layout has issues: buttons can overlap on small screens, no landscape/portrait adaptation, and there is no sprint button defined.

This means the game is completely unplayable on mobile devices.

title: Consolidate audio param factory tests
description:
engine/audio.test.js has ~25 tests for get*Params functions (getFootstepParams, getClimbingGrabParams, getHeartbeatParams, getSwordSlashParams, getColossusImpactParams, getWeakPointHitParams, getColossusDeathParams, getAmbientWindParams) that mostly check 'does this function return an object with these properties' and 'is charged/destroyed bigger than normal'. These are glorified config lookups.

Consolidate each get*Params function to: 1 structural test (has required fields) + 1 behavioral test (charged vs normal comparison). Keep the shouldPlayHeartbeat and shouldPlayFootstep tests as they have real timer logic.

Expected: ~20 tests removed, ~8 tests kept for param functions.
acceptance_criteria: Each get*Params function has at most 2 tests. shouldPlayHeartbeat/shouldPlayFootstep unchanged. node --test passes.

title: Use fixedStep() instead of step() for consistent physics
description:
**Problem:** engine/cannon-adapter.js uses world.step(clampedDt, undefined, 3) which requires manual time accumulation.

**cannon-es best practice:** Use world.fixedStep() which automatically handles time accumulation and runs at consistent rate independent of framerate.

Current: internal.cannonWorld.step(clampedDt, undefined, 3)

Should be: internal.cannonWorld.fixedStep() (uses default 1/60s timestep, max 10 substeps)

Or with custom: fixedStep(1/60, 1/60, 3)

title: Climbing normal never updates on curved surfaces
description:
In player/climbing.js line ~70, once grabbed, the climbNormal is frozen at the initial grab point. On curved colossus surfaces (which all three colossi have), the player movement direction becomes increasingly wrong as they climb further. The player appears to slide sideways or struggle to move in the intended direction.

Fix: Recalculate the surface normal each frame based on the player's current position on the surface.

title: Implement debug mode
description: Create a debug mode that helps visualize and understand various game systems including physics, collision detection, performance metrics, and other failing systems.

title: Combat system uses direct mutation, inconsistent with project patterns
description: player/combat.js mutates state in place. All other systems use immutable spread. Makes combat untestable. Three separate places duplicate weak point mutation logic.

title: GDD says colossus health shown via fading opacity, but hud.js draws a health bar
description: GDD specifies 'Colossus health: Shown as fading opacity of the colossus itself (no bar)'. However engine/hud.js drawHealthBar() renders a traditional horizontal health bar at top-center. engine/ui.js correctly returns opacity data (getColossusHealthVisual), but it's never consumed. These two approaches conflict and the opacity-based approach from the GDD is not implemented.

title: Keep cape and particles as cosmetic-only (no physics engine)
description:
Decision issue: cape Verlet chain and world particles should NOT be migrated to cannon-es.

**Rationale:**
- Cape uses Verlet integration with distance constraints — well-suited for cloth, overkill for a physics engine
- Particles are purely cosmetic (visual dust/embers) with no collision
- Moving them to cannon-es would add overhead for no gameplay benefit
- They already work and have full test coverage

**Action items:**
1. Document this decision in a comment at the top of `player/cape.js` and `world/particles.js`
2. Wind forces on the player should still go through the adapter (wind is a force source, not a physics system)
3. Wind forces on particles can remain direct (particles are cosmetic)
4. Verify cape receives player position from the adapter (already does via state)

**No code changes beyond documentation comments.**

title: Player has full air control - no gravity-based arc on jumps
description: In player/movement.js, applyMovement applies horizontal velocity regardless of grounded state. The player can steer mid-air exactly as on the ground. There's no momentum-based jumping - jumps feel floaty and unnatural. Combined with no air control reduction, the player can change direction 180 degrees mid-jump.

title: Voxel Direction Indicators and HUD Adaptation

## Requirements

2. Consider voxel-styled indicators (stack of small voxels forming arrow).
3. HUD: 2D canvas overlay is unaffected by voxel migration. No changes needed.
4. UI (engine/ui.js): DOM-based, unaffected.

title: Mouse attack input never works via isPressed('attack')
description:
In engine/input.js, isPressed('attack') maps to 'Mouse0' but mouse clicks are never added to the justPressed/prevPressed tracking arrays. Only keyboard codes go there. Mouse is tracked separately via the mouseButton field. This means any code using isPressed('attack') will never detect mouse clicks as attacks.

Also: mouseButton is a single int value, so pressing left+right simultaneously only tracks the last one. Releasing either button clears to 0.

title: Voxel Colossus Meshing: Replace procedural geometry with voxel-built colossi
description:
## Problem
All three colossi currently use primitive Three.js geometries (boxes, spheres, cylinders, cones, custom BufferGeometry for wings). Each colossus is a hierarchy of parts in a Three.js Group. This must be replaced with voxel representations.

## Requirements
1. Define each colossus as a 3D voxel template (or set of voxel primitives: voxel box, voxel sphere, voxel cylinder, voxel hull).
2. Stone Sentinel (scale 20, 12 parts): Quadruped golem with box body, cylinder legs, head, glowing rune weak points.
3. Wind Wraith (scale 30, 9 parts): Serpentine flyer with hemisphere segments, tapered wings, horns. Semi-transparent voxel support.
4. Tide Titan (scale 40, 17 parts): Turtle-crab with hemisphere shell, underbelly, head, claws, legs. Shell tilt animation.
5. Part hierarchy must be preserved for: climbing surfaces, weak point positioning, animation, death animation per-part transforms.
6. Surface patch generation must work on voxel faces instead of smooth geometry.
7. LOD: simplified voxel mesh at distance (fewer blocks, or box fallback).
8. Animation: voxel parts must rotate/translate/animate same as current system.

## Affected files
- New: colossus/voxel-builder.js, colossus/voxel-templates.js
- Modified: colossus/sentinel.js, colossus/wraith.js, colossus/titan.js, colossus/integration.js, colossus/base.js
- Depends on: polyshadow-t1g (voxel engine core)

title: Merge overly granular factory property tests
description:
Several test files test factory functions with one test per property instead of one test checking all properties:

1. world/particles.test.js: createParticle has 5 separate tests (position, velocity, lifetime, maxLifetime, size) — merge into 1
2. player/combat.test.js: findHitWeakPoint duplicates filtering logic already covered by trySlash — reduce from 5 to 2-3
3. colossus/sentinel.test.js: 'total patch count is reasonable' tests arbitrary bounds (>100 && <2000) — remove
4. game/progression.test.js: 'progress.defeated is a Set' tests implementation detail — remove
5. engine/input.test.js: minor overlap in WD/Arrow normalization and isTouchInButtonRegion center/inside — trim 2

Expected: ~10 tests removed.
acceptance_criteria: createParticle tested in 1 test. findHitWeakPoint reduced to 2-3 tests. Arbitrary bound checks removed. Implementation detail tests removed. node --test passes.

title: Sky dome uses DoubleSide instead of BackSide, wasting GPU
description: In world/sky.js line ~11, the sky dome uses side: 2 (THREE.DoubleSide) which renders both inner and outer faces, doubling fragment shader work. Since the camera is always inside the dome, BackSide would be correct and significantly cheaper. This impacts performance especially on mobile.

title: Build script missing cannon-es in import map
description: In build.js, the generated polyshadow.html has an import map that only includes three and three/addons, but the bundled code imports cannon-es. This causes 'Failed to resolve module specifier cannon-es' error at runtime. The index.html correctly includes cannon-es in the import map but the build script overwrites it. Fix build.js to include cannon-es in the import map (line ~239 add "cannon-es": "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/cannon-es@0.20.0/dist/cannon-es.js").

title: Voxel Player Character: Replace low-poly humanoid mesh with voxel player
description:
## Problem
Player character (player/character-mesh.js) is currently 7 box meshes + cape plane + sword group at 1.5x scale (~2.1 units tall). Must become a voxel figure.

## Requirements
1. Define player as a small voxel template (roughly 3-5 blocks tall, 1-2 blocks wide).
2. Player body: voxel torso, head, arms, legs matching current color scheme (tan torso, dark brown limbs, gray sword).
3. Cape: array of voxel columns that follow verlet cloth simulation (adapt cape.js to work with voxel cape segments).
4. Sword: voxel blade+hilt attached to player group.
5. Player physics body (cannon-es capsule) may need adjustment to match voxel dimensions.
6. Animation: limb movement while walking (simple voxel group rotations).

## Affected files
- Modified: player/character-mesh.js, player/cape.js
- Depends on: polyshadow-t1g (voxel engine core)

title: Phase 6: Polish - Audio, music, post-processing, ending, mobile, gamepad, UI
description: Procedural audio system (all sounds), layered music system, post-processing (bloom, color grading), win state / ending sequence, mobile touch overlay, gamepad support, UI refinement (stamina arc, title card, credits)

title: Procedural audio: all sound effects via Web Audio API
description: Implement all sound effects using Web Audio API (engine/audio.js). Ambient wind (filtered noise + LFO), footsteps (noise bursts + bandpass), climbing grabs (filtered clicks), stamina low (heartbeat pulse), sword slash (metallic resonance), colossus impact (low-freq boom), weak point hit (resonant ding + reverb), colossus death (evolving drone).

title: Layered music system with crossfading between game states
description: Implement layered ambient music. Base layer (always playing, warm drone), exploration layer (adds when roaming), combat layer (adds rhythmic pulse + tension near colossus), victory layer (ascending harmony after final kill). All layers crossfade smoothly between states.

title: Win state, ending sequence, and credits
description: After defeating all 3 colossi: sky opens up revealing cosmic vista, islands rise and converge, final silent scene with camera and world, wanderer at convergence point, title card fade in, credits roll while player can still move. TDD-test progression state machine (game/state.js, game/progression.js).

title: Mobile touch overlay and gamepad support
description: Extend input system for mobile and gamepad. Mobile: virtual joystick (left), touch drag look (right), jump/grab/attack buttons (bottom right), semi-transparent, auto-detected via touch events. Gamepad: left stick move, right stick look, standard button mappings. TDD-test gamepad input mapping and touch zone calculations.
notes: Implemented mobile touch overlay and gamepad support via TDD (45 new tests). Pure functions: classifyTouchZone, calculateJoystickVector, normalizeStick, mapGamepadButtons, mapGamepadToInput, isTouchInButtonRegion. Classes: TouchInputManager (virtual joystick, touch look, action buttons), GamepadInputManager (stick/button mapping with configurable dead zones and sensitivity). All 62 tests pass.

title: UI: stamina arc, title screen, minimal HUD, and post-processing
description: Implement minimal UI (engine/ui.js): stamina bar as thin arc around player (white to red), colossus health shown as fading opacity (no bar), direction hints (faint light pillars). Title screen: game title on blurred golden sky, instant gameplay start. Post-processing: bloom on weak points, color grading. No pause menu beyond simple resume/quit overlay.

title: Voxel Audio Integration: Adapt procedural audio for voxel context

## Problem
Audio system (engine/audio.js, engine/music.js) is abstracted from visuals and uses Web Audio API procedural synthesis. Most audio should work as-is, but some sound triggers may need adaptation.

## Requirements
1. Footstep sounds: Currently trigger based on ground contact. With voxel terrain, consider per-block-type footstep sounds (grass vs stone vs dirt).
2. Climbing sounds: Currently filtered_click. No change needed — still grab-based.
3. Combat sounds: No change needed — still proximity/weak-point based.
4. Colossus sounds: No change needed — still state-machine driven.
5. Ambient wind: No change needed.
6. Optional: Add block break/place sounds if voxel modification is implemented.

## Affected files
- Modified: engine/audio.js (add block-type footstep mapping)
- Depends on: polyshadow-t1g (voxel core)

title: Voxel Physics: Adapt collision system for voxel terrain
description:
## Problem
Current physics uses cannon-es with Trimesh colliders for island terrain (built from heightmap grid geometry). Voxel terrain requires a different collision approach.

## Requirements
1. **Terrain collision**: Replace heightmap-based ground height queries (getGroundHeight / bilinear interpolation) with voxel raycasting or height queries against voxel chunks.
2. **Island physics colliders**: Replace Trimesh colliders (island-physics.js) with voxel-based colliders. Options:
   a. Heightfield collider per chunk (cannon-es supports Heightfield shapes)
   b. Voxel shell collision (only surface voxels)
   c. AABB per-chunk with detailed voxel collision on contact
3. **Colossus collision**: Colossus kinematic bodies currently use box shapes. Voxel colossi need colliders that match their voxel shape — likely compound shapes or convex hulls generated from voxel shell.
4. **Climbing collision**: Surface patches must be generated from voxel faces (exposed solid faces) instead of computed from smooth geometry patches.
5. **Stepping stone collision**: Replace isOnPath() proximity check with voxel column collision queries.
6. Player capsule vs voxel terrain interaction must feel correct (no falling through cracks between voxels).

## Affected files
- Modified: engine/cannon-adapter.js, world/island-physics.js, player/climbing.js, world/island.js
- Depends on: polyshadow-t1g (voxel core), polyshadow-tsv (voxel islands)

title: No rest spots on colossus bodies for stamina regeneration

title: Player slides away from respawn point after dying/falling
description:
In game/main.js lines ~627-634, respawn() teleports the player to the spawn point, but updatePlayer() runs immediately after in the same frame. If any movement keys are held (or stuck), the player slides away from the spawn point before they can react. Combined with no invincibility frames, this can cause chain deaths.

Fix: Clear movement input after respawn, or skip movement update on the respawn frame.

title: No collision detection - player walks through all geometry
description: player/movement.js has no collision against islands, colossi, or world objects. Player can walk through everything. Only ground height snapping exists. physics-adapter has AABB-only with numerous bugs. Player exists in empty world with height maps.

title: Voxel Materials and Texturing: Per-block-face materials for visual variety
description:
## Problem
Current materials use MeshStandardMaterial with vertex colors and procedural normal maps. Voxel rendering needs a different approach for block-type visual variety.

## Requirements
1. **Block face materials**: Each block type needs distinct face textures/colors (grass top vs dirt sides, stone faces, moss edges, glowing runes).
2. **Texture atlas or vertex colors**: Options:
   a. Texture atlas with UV mapping per face in the greedy mesh
   b. Vertex colors in the greedy mesh (simpler, no textures needed, fits zero-build constraint)
   c. Canvas-generated textures per block type
3. **Normal mapping**: Adapt or remove procedural normal maps (utils/normal-map.js). Voxel faces are flat — normal maps add surface detail but may not fit the voxel aesthetic.
4. **Weak point glow**: Emissive voxel faces for colossus weak points (replace current emissive MeshStandardMaterial + PointLights).
5. **Transparent blocks**: Semi-transparent voxel support for Wind Wraith (opacity 0.65-0.85).
6. **Performance**: Single draw call per material group if possible (batch voxel faces by block type).

## Affected files
- Modified: world/voxel-mesher.js, colossus/weak-point-visuals.js, colossus/health-visual.js, engine/renderer.js
- Deprecated: utils/normal-map.js
- Depends on: polyshadow-t1g (voxel core)

title: Island terrain not added to physics world - no ground collision
description: In game/main.js lines 447-465, island meshes are created and added to the Three.js scene, but no corresponding physics bodies are added to the physics world. The physics adapter has createTrimeshCollider() capability but it's never called for islands. Player body falls through the ground because there's no collision. Need to add: for each island, call physicsAdapter.createTrimeshCollider() with the island geometry and physicsAdapter.addBody() to add it as a static body.

description: engine/camera.js collisionLayers stored but never used. Camera clips through islands, colossi. GDD specifies 'Collision avoidance' and 'Auto-adjusts distance based on context'. Neither implemented. No snapToTarget method causes lerp glitch during arena teleport.

title: Replace player movement physics with cannon-es adapter
description:
Refactor `player/movement.js` to use the physics adapter instead of manual Euler integration.

**Changes:**
- `applyMovement`: call `adapter.setVelocity(body, {x, y, z})` instead of mutating state.velocity directly
- `applyJump`: call `adapter.applyImpulse(body, {x: 0, y: JUMP_FORCE, z: 0})` instead of setting velocity.y
- `applyGravity`: REMOVE entirely — cannon-es handles gravity natively
- `updatePlayer`: call `adapter.getPosition(body)` to read back position after step
- Ground collision: handled by cannon-es trimesh collider on island mesh, read `isGrounded` from contact events
- Keep all existing function signatures — the adapter is an implementation detail

**TDD:** Existing movement tests must still pass. Add tests verifying the adapter is called correctly (mock the adapter).

**Files:** player/movement.js, player/movement.test.js

title: Replace fall and climbing physics with adapter
description:
Refactor `player/fall.js` and `player/climbing.js` to use the physics adapter.

**fall.js:**
- `enterFall`: use `adapter.setVelocity` to zero Y, set gravity multiplier via adapter
- `updateFall`: REMOVE manual gravity integration — read position from adapter after step. Fall-specific gravity can be a force scale applied per-frame
- `respawn`: `adapter.setPosition` + `adapter.setVelocity(zero)`

**climbing.js:**
- `tryGrab`: `adapter.setPosition` to snap to surface + `adapter.setVelocity(zero)` + set body type to kinematic while climbing
- `applyClimbingMovement`: `adapter.setPosition` for direct control while kinematic
- `tryJumpClimb`: `adapter.setPosition` for snap between surfaces
- `releaseGrab`: set body type back to dynamic, adapter handles falling naturally

**TDD:** Existing fall/climbing tests must pass. Add adapter mock tests.

**Files:** player/fall.js, player/fall.test.js, player/climbing.js, player/climbing.test.js

title: Island height variation not respected - player collides with plane bottom
description:
The islands have height variation (hills, valleys) created by island.js, but the player's ground collision uses a flat plane approximation. When there's a hill, the player still collides with the lowest point of the island's bounding plane instead of the actual surface height.

Current behavior:
- Islands are generated with heightData containing varied terrain
- getIslandSurfaceHeight() exists and returns correct surface height
- But player ground collision uses flat plane/groundHeight logic

Expected behavior:
- Player should collide with the actual island surface at their X,Z position
- Hills should block movement, not be walked over
- Valleys should be accessible

Related to: polyshadow-dtp (collision detection)

The tutorial-overlay div exists in HTML but has no handler to show it. According to the GDD and HTML (line 300-321), the tutorial should display controls (WASD, Space, E, Click) when the game starts. Currently player jumps straight into gameplay with no onboarding.

title: Colossus AI: behavior state machine (idle, patrol, aggro, stunned, dying)

title: Climbable surfaces and weak points on colossus body

description: Implement combat system (player/combat.js). Sword slash attacks weak points only. Stab is charged attack while climbing. Colossus periodically shakes to throw player off (extra stamina drain to hold on). TDD-test damage calculation, weak point hit detection, shake-off timing.

title: Colossus death animation and arena island for Stone Sentinel

title: tryJumpClimb has no cooldown - holding jump teleports infinitely
description:
In player/climbing.js, tryJumpClimb has no cooldown. If the player holds the jump button, they teleport to the nearest climbable surface every single frame. Also no stamina check is performed - infinite teleporting at 0 stamina. findNearestClimbableSurface ignores player facing direction - can grab surfaces behind them.

Fix: Add a cooldown (e.g. 300ms), require jump release before re-trigger, and check stamina before allowing jump-climb.

title: No rest spots on colossus bodies for stamina regeneration
description: GDD specifies 'Rest spots: Flat areas on colossus where stamina regenerates'. No such system exists. When climbing, stamina drains constantly even without moving (player/stamina.js updateStamina calls drainStamina while climbing regardless of input). The player must choose between falling and progressing - there's no tactical stamina management via rest spots.

title: Fix lint warnings in colossus/wraith.test.js
description:
Fix 2 issues:
- Remove unused import `getAllClimbableParts` from base.js (line 22)
- Fix erasing operation bug: `0 * 1.5 + 1 * 0.8` evaluates to 0.8 but the `0 * 1.5` is a no-op (line 893). Likely should be a variable reference.

title: Sky dome uses DoubleSide instead of BackSide, wastes GPU
description: world/sky.js: sky dome uses side: 2 (THREE.DoubleSide) rendering both faces, doubling fragment work. Camera always inside dome so BackSide is correct and cheaper. Impacts mobile performance.

title: Input manager has duplicate event handler - mouseup handler overwritten
description: In engine/input.js line 1045-1047, there are two definitions of this._onMouseUp. The first one properly deletes from pressedMouseButtons, but the second one (lines 1045-1047) just resets the entire Set to empty: this.pressedMouseButtons = new Set(). This overwrites the proper handler and loses all button state. The second definition is a bug and should be removed.

title: Camera doesn't follow player movement
description: The orbit camera is initialized with a fixed target position (0, 0, 0) and never updates to follow the player's position. When the player moves, the camera stays at the origin looking at empty space. The orbit.snapToTarget() call exists (line 1229) but may not be working correctly, or the camera isn't being synced to player position each frame. Player appears to be stationary because camera doesn't track them.

title: Phase 7: Jam Prep - Optimization, testing, deploy, submit

title: Performance optimization: LOD, batching, occlusion, shadow quality
description: Optimize for 60fps desktop / 30fps mobile. LOD system for distant geometry. Batch static geometry into merged buffers. Occlusion culling for islands not in view. Single directional shadow map, low resolution, cascade for close range. Distance fog to hide pop-in. TDD-test LOD selection logic and culling thresholds.

title: Integrate input: gamepad and touch overlay support into main loop

title: Integrate game systems: state machine, progression, UI/HUD, audio, music, camera

Need title screen, game state transitions, colossus defeat tracking, stamina arc HUD, post-processing config, procedural sound effects, layered music with crossfading. title: Wire game state machine into main loop
description: Create GameState instance. On title: show title screen, wait for input. On playing: run game. On victory: play ending sequence (5s). On credits: roll credits (15s). On paused: show pause overlay. transition() on events. Call update(dt) each frame for timed transitions.

title: Wire progression tracker with colossus defeats

title: Add HUD rendering: stamina arc, colossus health, direction hints

title: Add title screen with game title and start prompt

title: Wire procedural audio into game events
description: Create Web Audio AudioContext. On player movement: getFootstepParams + shouldPlayFootstep. On climbing grab: getClimbingGrabParams. Low stamina heartbeat: shouldPlayHeartbeat + getHeartbeatParams. Combat: getSwordSlashParams, getWeakPointHitParams. Colossus impact: getColossusImpactParams. Death: getColossusDeathParams. Ambient wind: getAmbientWindParams.

title: Add post-processing: bloom, vignette, color grading

title: Add ending sequence: sky opening, islands converging, credits

Camera pans up. After 5s, credits text fades in. Player can still move during credits. After 15s, return to title.

Currently game/main.js only has a player box and sky. Need to generate floating islands around the player, add volumetric fog, particle systems, wind effects, and colossus direction indicators. Create a createIslandMesh(island) function in renderer.js that builds a PlaneGeometry from heightData with vertex colors (brown/green/stone). Apply wind force to particles and player movement (slight push). Visualize currents as faint flowing line particles.

title: Add bridge/stepping stone paths between islands
description: Integrate world/paths.js. Create paths connecting hub island to each arena island. Use generatePathPoints for stepping stones. Render as flat stone meshes floating between islands. Player can walk on them (ground collision with path height sampling).

title: Add fog layers as volumetric particle planes
description: Integrate world/fog.js. Create semi-transparent fog planes at 3 default layer heights. Use updateFogSystem each frame, adjust plane opacity via calculateFogDensity at camera height.

title: Integrate colossi: spawn, AI behavior, death sequence, climbing surfaces

Create box/cylinder geometries for each body part (torso, hips, head, 4 legs, 2 back runes, 2 arm runes). Scale by STONE_SENTINEL_SCALE=20. Apply stone material (dark gray with slight vertex color variation). Position parts relative to parent hierarchy. Animate idle breathing (subtle scale pulse).

title: Create procedural 3D meshes for Tide Titan
description: Build Three.js meshes from titan definition (colossus/titan.js createTitanDefinition). Large shell (dome), underbelly, head, 2 claws, 4 legs, 2 runes. Scale by TIDE_TITAN_SCALE=40. Shell has rocky texture, underbelly smoother. Animate shell tilt (getShellTilt), leg movement, claw snapping.

title: Create procedural 3D meshes for Wind Wraith
description: Build Three.js meshes from wraith definition (colossus/wraith.js createWraithDefinition). Serpentine body: chain of spheres/segments for neck, chest, tail sections. Wings as flat triangulated planes. Head with horns. Scale by WIND_WRAITH_SCALE=30. Pale/translucent material. Animate wing flap and serpentine undulation.

title: Create procedural 3D meshes for Stone Sentinel
description: Build Three.js meshes from sentinel definition (colossus/sentinel.js). Box/cylinder geometries for 12 body parts. Scale by 20. Stone material. Animate idle breathing.

title: Create procedural 3D meshes for Tide Titan

title: Wire colossus AI behavior state machines into update loop
description: Run updateBehavior (sentinel), updateWraithBehavior, updateTitanBehavior each frame. Pass player position, colossus position, dt. Implement state transitions (idle->patrol->aggro->stunned). Move colossus meshes based on AI position output. Trigger shake-off when shouldShakeOff. Handle shouldAttack for damage to player.

title: Implement climbing on colossus surfaces
description: Use colossus/surface.js patches + player/climbing.js. When player is near colossus and presses action, findNearestClimbableSurface. Attach player to colossus body part. Move player with applyClimbingMovement along surface. Drain stamina via player/stamina.js. Show climbing patches as glowing areas on colossus mesh. Player mesh follows colossus movement while attached.

title: Implement death sequence with dissolve animation
description: When all weak points destroyed, trigger triggerDeath. Run updateDeathAnimation each frame. Apply getPartTransform to each body part mesh (kneel, collapse, dissolve). Fade opacity, offset positions, shake camera via getDeathShakeIntensity. When isDeathComplete, call progression.defeatColossus and remove colossus from scene.

title: Add weak point glow visuals and hit feedback
description: Show weak points as glowing runes on colossus body (use buildCombatWeakPoints positions). Render as small emissive sphere meshes. On hit (trySlash/tryStab), flash white then fade. Destroyed weak points turn dark. Bloom post-processing highlights active weak points.

title: Integrate player systems: climbing, stamina, combat, cape, falling
description: Wire player/climbing.js, player/stamina.js, player/combat.js, player/cape.js, player/fall.js into game/main.js. Player needs to climb colossi surfaces with stamina drain, attack weak points, cape physics, and fall damage. All logic tested but NOT in render loop.

title: Replace box player with procedural character mesh
description: Replace the current box mesh with a humanoid figure: head (sphere), body (box), arms (thin boxes), legs (thin boxes), cape (line segments from player/cape.js). Apply updateChain each frame for cape Verlet simulation. Material: dark cloth with vertex color variation.

title: Integrate stamina system with climbing and sprinting
description: Add stamina state (createStaminaState) to player. Run updateStamina each frame with isClimbing/isSprinting flags. Drain on sprint (SPRINT_DRAIN_RATE=15) and climb (CLIMB_DRAIN_RATE=12). Regen when grounded and not climbing. Pass stamina to UI for arc display.

title: Integrate combat: slash, stab, weak point damage

Apply damage via applyDamageToWeakPoint. Cooldowns via updateCombat. Trigger audio (getSwordSlashParams, getWeakPointHitParams).

title: Add fall damage and respawn system

title: Add colossus arena loading and transitions

title: Remove structural property-enumeration tests in colossus files
description:
titan.test.js, wraith.test.js, and sentinel.test.js have ~30 tests that enumerate properties of returned objects without testing logic: 'each patch has the correct format' (12 individual typeof checks repeated across 3 files), 'createBehaviorState returns default state' (checking 14-17 fields of an object literal spread), 'buildCombatWeakPoints each has id/position/health...', 'has the expected part ids' (listing every part from the definition just created).

Replace repetitive patch format validation with a shared validatePatchFormat() helper called once per test file. Trim default state tests to check 2-3 key fields. Remove 'has expected part ids' tests since they just re-read the data that was just constructed. Keep tests that verify logic: normals are unit vectors, health multipliers are correct, climbable/non-climbable classification, geometry type per part.

Expected: ~20 tests removed, 1 shared helper added.
acceptance_criteria: No test file has inline 12-line typeof checks for patch format. Default state tests check max 3 key fields. No 'has expected part ids' enumeration tests. node --test passes.

title: Missing favicon causes 404 on every page load

title: Create pluggable physics adapter abstraction layer
description:
Design and implement `engine/physics-adapter.js` — a thin abstraction over the physics engine (cannon-es) that allows swapping implementations.

**Interface:**
- `createPhysicsWorld()` — returns world instance
- `createBody(opts)` — create rigid body (dynamic, kinematic, static)
- `createTrimeshCollider(geometry)` — static mesh collider for islands
- `addBody(body)` / `removeBody(body)`
- `step(dt)` — tick the simulation
- `applyForce(body, force)` / `applyImpulse(body, impulse)`
- `setVelocity(body, vec)` / `getVelocity(body)`
- `setPosition(body, vec)` / `getPosition(body)`
- `raycast(from, to)` — returns hit or null
- `onCollision(bodyA, bodyB, callback)` — collision events
- `body.userData` — arbitrary data on bodies

**Implementation:** `engine/cannon-adapter.js` using cannon-es loaded via CDN (`esm.sh/cannon-es`).

**TDD:** Test the adapter interface with a mock physics backend. The adapter itself is a thin wrapper — logic stays in game modules, not in the adapter.

**IMPORTANT:** Do NOT import cannon-es directly in any game logic file. All physics access goes through the adapter.

title: Ground detection uses manual raycasts instead of physics contacts
description:
**Problem:** Ground detection in player/collision.js uses manual raycasts instead of cannon-es native collision detection.

Current uses raycasts down 3 units. Should use collision events or world.contacts array.

Per cannon-es docs: Use collision events body.addEventListener('collide', callback) or check world.contacts after step. Contact normal.y > 0.3 indicates ground contact.

title: isClimbing state inconsistency within single frame in main loop
description: game/main.js ~598-614: isClimbing captures OLD state at line 598, climbing updated at 603, but line 614 uses NEW state. prevClimbing at 606 uses OLD value but rest of frame uses NEW. Causes one-frame glitches in audio/particles/camera response.

title: Mouse events fire outside pointer lock, can trigger attacks on UI
description: In engine/input.js _onMouseDown (line ~92), there's no check for document.pointerLockElement. Clicking UI elements (pause menu, title screen) sets mouseButton, potentially triggering sword attacks. The attack should only register while the pointer is locked to the game canvas.

title: Voxel Editor/World Modification: Runtime block placement and destruction
description:
## Problem
Moving to voxels opens the possibility of runtime world modification (block placement/destruction). This isn't in the original GDD but is core to the voxel aesthetic and could enhance gameplay (e.g., colossus attacks destroy terrain blocks, weak point hits destroy colossus voxels).

## Requirements
1. **Block destruction**: Remove individual voxels (e.g., when weak point is hit, destroy surrounding voxels; colossus attacks crater terrain).
2. **Block placement**: Optionally allow player to place blocks (if desired for gameplay).
3. **Chunk dirty tracking**: Modified chunks must be flagged dirty and remeshed.
4. **Physics sync**: Destroyed blocks must update collision (remove collider or mark as air in collision queries).
5. **Climbing sync**: If colossus voxels are destroyed, climbing surface patches must regenerate.
6. **Performance**: Frequent small modifications should batch remeshing to avoid frame drops.

## Affected files
- New: world/voxel-modifier.js
- Modified: world/voxel-storage.js, world/voxel-mesher.js, colossus/weak-point-visuals.js
- Depends on: polyshadow-t1g (voxel core), polyshadow-qkl (voxel climbing)

title: Tide Titan leg positions drift infinitely upward due to additive animation bug
description:
In colossus/titan.js line ~698, leg animation uses additive positioning: leg.position.y = leg.position.y + Math.sin(phase) * 0.3. This ADDS to Y each frame instead of SETTING it relative to the original position. Legs will drift upward or downward without bound over time, eventually flying off or sinking into the ground.

Fix: Store original leg positions at creation and set position relative to them (like wraith.js does).

title: Combat system uses direct mutation, inconsistent with project patterns
description: player/combat.js uses direct mutation throughout (trySlash, tryStab, updateCombat all mutate state in place). Every other system in the project uses immutable spread patterns. This makes combat untestable and causes subtle bugs when state is shared. Also, trySlash and tryStab duplicate weak point mutation logic in three separate places.

title: Improve ember particle visual quality
description: Current embers are tiny dots (PointsMaterial size: 0.15). Larger, softer particles with size attenuation and maybe a glow texture would look much better. Also, bloom would make them glow more.

title: Climbing normal never updates on curved colossus surfaces
description: player/climbing.js freezes climbNormal at initial grab point. On curved surfaces (all colossi), movement direction becomes wrong as player climbs further. Player appears to slide sideways or struggle. Fix: recalculate normal each frame.

title: Physics adapter has no friction - player slides endlessly on slopes
description: engine/physics-adapter.js implements zero friction and zero restitution between bodies. The player slides on any sloped surface with no deceleration. This makes walking on hilly terrain feel like ice skating.

title: [bug] Player character too small and blends in - hard to track position
description:
## Description
The player character is very small (0.5x0.7 torso) and blends in with the environment. It's hard to tell where the player is and whether they're moving.

The character appears as a tiny figure in the bottom-right of the screen.

## Expected

title: GDD says colossus health via fading opacity but hud.js draws a health bar

title: Audio nodes never garbage collected - potential memory leak
description: In game/main.js, playProceduralSound creates oscillators, buffer sources, filters, and gain nodes on every call. The cleanupSounds function only tracks metadata (activeSounds map), not actual Web Audio nodes. Over extended play sessions, this could cause memory pressure and audio degradation, especially on mobile.

title: Voxel Climbing System: Adapt surface patches for voxel colossus faces
description:
## Problem
Current climbing system (player/climbing.js) works with surface patches — grid of grab points computed from colossus body part geometry faces. Voxel colossi have block-aligned faces, not smooth geometry.

## Requirements
1. **Surface patch generation**: Generate grab points from exposed voxel faces on colossus surface. Each exposed solid face = potential grab point.
2. **Grab detection**: Find nearest exposed voxel face within grab distance (current max grab distance ~2.5).
3. **Surface-relative movement**: Project movement input onto voxel face normals (only 6 directions: +X, -X, +Y, -Y, +Z, -Z instead of arbitrary normals).
4. **Jump-climb**: Find reachable exposed voxel faces for wall-leaping between surfaces. Must handle axis-aligned jumps.
5. **Rest spots**: Flat voxel surfaces on colossus where stamina regenerates. Must identify flat horizontal voxel surfaces (top faces of blocks).
6. **Surface patch grouping**: Group voxel faces by body part for colossus part tracking.
7. **Voxel destruction during climbing**: If voxels are destroyed (weak point hit), climbing surfaces must update dynamically.

## Affected files
- Modified: player/climbing.js, player/climbing-integration.js, colossus/surface.js, colossus/rest-spots.js
- Depends on: polyshadow-8bq (voxel colossi), polyshadow-bov (voxel physics)

title: Particle system uses wall-clock time and frame-rate-dependent physics
description:
In world/particles.js:
1. performance.now() is used for time (line 57, 93) instead of game time. If the game pauses or tab-switches, particles behave incorrectly.
2. Velocity integration is frame-rate dependent (line 70-72). The implicit *60 factor assumes 60fps. At 30fps particles move half speed per frame; at 120fps they jitter.
3. Respawned particles inherit their random lifetime as maxLifetime instead of the default, breaking fade-out ratio calculations.

title: Cape physics: chain constraint resolves at half intended length
description:
In player/cape.js solveChainConstraints (line ~74), the 50/50 correction split means node 0 (pinned) never moves but node b only moves by 0.5*diff instead of the full diff. This makes the cape chain always half the intended rest length when under tension, causing the cape to look stretched/compressed.

Also: Wind force uses dt*dt which makes wind nearly negligible at 60fps (0.016^2 = 0.00027). Cape barely reacts to wind.

title: Mouse events fire outside pointer lock
description: engine/input.js: _onMouseDown has no document.pointerLockElement check. Clicking UI triggers attacks.

title: Increase default camera distance
description: The camera is currently too close to the player. Need to increase the default distance in the OrbitCamera class (currently set to 10).

title: Phase 5: Remaining Colossi - Wind Wraith and Tide Titan
description: Wind Wraith: flying behavior, aerial combat, swoop-grab mechanic. Tide Titan: multi-phase, water hazards, tilting shell. Unique arenas for each.

title: Wind Wraith: serpentine flying colossus with swoop-grab and aerial combat
description: Implement Wind Wraith (colossus/wraith.js). Serpentine/dragon-like flying creature, ~30x player length. Flies in patterns, swoops to attack. Player must jump onto it from elevated platforms. Wind pushes player. 2 weak points on wings, 1 on neck. Open sky arena with floating platforms. TDD-test flight AI state machine, swoop timing, and wind push forces.

title: Tide Titan: turtle-crab hybrid with multi-phase fight and water hazards
description: Implement Tide Titan (colossus/titan.js). Massive turtle-crab hybrid, ~40x player width. Slow but devastating, shockwaves, submerges arena parts. Unstable climbing surface with tilting shell. 3 weak points on shell, 1 on head (only exposed when stunned). Multi-phase fight. Cloud-covered arena with rising/falling pools. TDD-test multi-phase state machine, tilt physics, and submersion timing.

title: [bug] Weak points not visually distinct - impossible to find without knowing where to look
description:
## Description
Weak points on colossi are not visually distinct from the rest of the body. Players cannot tell where to attack without prior knowledge or extensive searching.

## Expected
Weak points should glow, pulse, or have some visual indicator to help players find them.

## Notes
Players need to know to look for weak points, but the current implementation gives no visual feedback.

title: Replace island geometry with boxes and add physics
description: Confirmed: islands use BufferGeometry with custom terrain, not flat planes. However, the collision geometry should use BoxGeometry with cannon-es physics bodies for proper collision detection with players and colossi.

title: Fix lint warnings in player/cape.test.js

Fix 2 issues:

title: Release grab gives no velocity - no jump-dismount from colossus
description: In player/climbing.js releaseGrab (line ~156), the player just stops and lets gravity take over. There's no jump-dismount or momentum preservation. In Shadow of the Colossus (the inspiration), releasing grab has a directional leap. Currently, releasing from a colossus just drops you straight down, which feels bad and makes it hard to reach intended landing spots.

title: Voxel Lighting and Atmosphere: Adapt sky, fog, god rays, bloom for voxel world
description:
## Problem
Current atmospheric systems (sky dome, fog layers, god rays, post-processing bloom) are tuned for smooth geometry. Voxel aesthetics may require adjustments.

## Requirements
1. **Sky dome**: May work as-is (sphere geometry, not terrain). But sky colors should complement the voxel aesthetic. Consider harder edges or more stylized palette.
2. **Fog system**: FogExp2 + layered fog planes (world/fog.js) should work but may need density/color tuning for voxel visibility distances.
3. **God rays**: Custom ShaderMaterial cylinders (world/god-rays.js) should work as-is but may look different. Consider voxel-aligned light shafts.
4. **Shadows**: PCFSoftShadowMap on voxel geometry — shadow quality on block edges. May need sharper shadow maps or voxel-style hard shadows.
5. **Bloom/post-processing**: Keep UnrealBloomPass but tune for emissive voxels (weak points, rune glow).
6. **Ambient occlusion**: Voxel AO — darkening voxels adjacent to other solid blocks. Can be baked into vertex colors during meshing (ambient occlusion per vertex based on neighboring solid blocks).

## Affected files
- Modified: world/sky.js, world/fog.js, world/god-rays.js, engine/post-processing.js, engine/renderer.js, world/voxel-mesher.js (AO)
- Depends on: polyshadow-t1g (voxel core), polyshadow-ehu (voxel materials)
notes: Implemented world/voxel-atmosphere.js with updateFogForVoxels, updateBloomForVoxels, createVoxelShadowConfig. Verified AO curve in voxel-mesher.js. 25 tests pass.

title: Voxel Engine Core: Chunk data structures, storage, and meshing pipeline
description:
## Problem
The entire game currently uses traditional Three.js geometries (BufferGeometry for terrain, BoxGeometry/SphereGeometry/CylinderGeometry for entities). Moving to voxels requires a foundational voxel engine layer.

## Requirements
1. Voxel data model: 3D sparse voxel storage (chunk-based). Each chunk = NxNxN block grid (likely 16x16x16 or 32x32x32). Support block types (air, dirt, grass, stone, moss_stone, etc.) with metadata.
2. Chunk manager: Load/unload chunks based on distance from camera/player. Pool/recycle chunks. Track dirty chunks needing remeshing.
3. Greedy meshing: Implement greedy meshing or contour meshing to generate optimal triangle geometry from voxel data. Must support per-block face culling.
4. Chunk mesh updates: Only remesh dirty chunks. Background meshing if possible.
5. Block palette: Define block types with properties: solid, transparent, color, emissive, etc.

## Affected files
- New: world/voxel-chunk.js, world/voxel-mesher.js, world/voxel-storage.js, world/block-types.js
- Modified: game/main.js (add voxel system init)
- Deprecated: engine/island-mesh.js, utils/procedural.js (heightmap generation replaced by voxel carving)

## Dependencies
None — foundation layer.

title: No stun-from-damage mechanic - wraith and titan cannot be stunned
description:
The GDD specifies a stun mechanic: 'Certain actions or environmental elements can stun a colossus, exposing weak points or creating climbing opportunities.' The sentinel has a basic stun, but:
- The Wind Wraith has no internal STUNNED transition in its behavior (updateWraithBehavior never transitions to STUNNED)
- There is no stun-from-damage mechanic anywhere - damaging weak points doesn't contribute to stunning
- The wraith's CLIMBING_BACK state is unclimbable and unattackable, giving free immunity

title: Voxel Island Generation: Replace heightmap islands with voxel-carved floating islands
description:
## Problem
Currently islands are generated as heightmap-based BufferGeometry (world/island.js, engine/island-mesh.js). Heights are stored in a 2D array and rendered as indexed triangle meshes. This must be replaced with voxel-based terrain.

## Requirements
1. Carve floating islands from voxel chunks using 3D noise functions (already have noise.js with fbm3D).
2. Generate island shapes: sphere/ellipsoid with FBM perturbation, then carve interior/edges for the floating look.
3. Surface voxel types: grass on top, dirt underneath, stone deep, mossy stone on edges/underside.
4. Hub island (radius ~60) and arena islands (radius ~40) must generate correctly.
5. Stepping stone paths between islands as voxel columns or small floating voxel formations.
6. Ruin generation: replace procedural ruin placement with voxel-structured ruins (walls, pillars made of voxel blocks).

## Affected files
- New: world/voxel-island-generator.js
- Modified: world/island.js (rewrite), world/paths.js (adapt for voxel paths)
- Deprecated: engine/island-mesh.js (replaced by voxel meshing)
- Depends on: polyshadow-t1g (voxel engine core)

title: Fix lint warnings in player/voxel-character-mesh.js
description: Remove unused import `isBlockSolid` from block-types.js (line 1).

title: gamepad-hint div has no CSS styling
description: In index.html line 169, the #gamepad-hint div exists but has no CSS rules. It renders as plain unstyled text at the top-left corner. It should be styled to match the game aesthetic - small text, positioned unobtrusively, with appropriate opacity and font.

title: Remove JS purity/idempotency and redundant coverage tests
description:
Several files have tests that verify JavaScript language semantics rather than game logic:

1. 'is pure function' tests (calling f(x) === f(x)) in island.test.js and indicators.test.js — ~4 tests
2. 'does not mutate the original state' tests that just verify spread operator works — fall.test.js, audio.test.js — ~4 tests  
3. Redundant filtering: 'fails on destroyed/inactive' tested in BOTH findHitWeakPoint AND trySlash/tryStab in combat.test.js — ~4 tests
4. Duplicate coverage: 'returns true at exact edge' + 'returns true at center' in isTouchInButtonRegion, 'returns false when exactly at threshold' + 'returns true when below threshold' overlaps in fall.test.js — ~6 tests

Keep immutability tests only where the function is non-trivial (fog.test.js createFogSystem, indicators.test.js updateIndicator). Keep one filtering test per pattern, not duplicated across functions.
acceptance_criteria: No 'is pure function' tests remain. Redundant filtering tests consolidated to one per pattern. Immutability tests only on non-trivial functions. node --test passes.

title: Phase 2: Climbing System - Grab, surface movement, stamina, cape physics, fall/respawn
description: Grab system (detect climbable surfaces), surface-relative movement, stamina system, cape physics (simple spring chain), climb on test geometry, fall + respawn

title: Stamina system with drain/regen/fall mechanics
description: Implement stamina system (player/ module). Climbing and sprinting drain stamina. Stamina regenerates when standing on ground or resting grip. Running out of stamina while climbing causes fall. Visual indicator: vignette pulse + cape stops flowing. TDD-test all stamina logic: drain rates, regen rates, thresholds, fall trigger.

title: Grab and climbable surface detection
description: Implement grab system: detect climbable surfaces near player, attach player to surface, snap to surface normal. Surface-relative movement while grabbed. Jump-climb between grab points. TDD-test surface detection logic, grab point validation, and snap-to-normal calculations.

title: Cape physics with simple spring chain simulation
description: Implement cape physics using a simple spring chain simulation. Cape should flow naturally with wind and movement, and stop flowing when stamina is low. TDD-test spring physics math (constraint solving, force accumulation).

title: Fall detection, freefall camera, and respawn system

title: Voxel LOD and Performance Optimization
description:
## Problem
Current LOD uses THREE.LOD with full mesh (distance < 30) and simplified box (30-80). Voxel rendering has unique LOD challenges — thousands of blocks per colossus and per island.

## Requirements
1. **Terrain LOD**: Reduce voxel resolution at distance. Options:
   a. Merge 2x2x2 blocks into single larger blocks (octree LOD).
   b. Skip chunks beyond render distance, use impostors.
2. **Colossus LOD**: Full voxel mesh up close, simplified voxel representation at distance (fewer blocks or box impostor).
3. **Render distance**: Configurable chunk render distance. Current game is small (single screen arenas) so this may not be critical.
4. **Meshing budget**: Limit mesh updates per frame. Queue dirty chunks, process N per frame.
5. **Draw call batching**: Ensure greedy meshing produces minimal draw calls. Group by material.
6. **Memory**: Sparse chunk storage — only allocate chunks that contain non-air blocks.
7. **Mobile optimization**: Reduce chunk size, render distance, and particle count (already handled in engine/lod.js but needs voxel-aware adaptation).

## Affected files
- Modified: engine/lod.js, world/voxel-mesher.js, world/voxel-storage.js, game/main.js
- Depends on: polyshadow-t1g (voxel core), polyshadow-tsv (voxel islands), polyshadow-8bq (voxel colossi)

title: Connect cape physics to visual mesh
description: The cape physics simulation exists in player/cape.js with full Verlet integration, but updateChain() is never called in the game loop. The cape renders as a static plane. Just calling it each frame and applying positions to the mesh would add immediate visual life.

title: Camera distance permanently shrinks during falls, never resets
description:
In game/main.js lines ~809-811, when the player enters fall state, the camera orbit distance is continuously multiplied by a zoom factor (orbit.setDistance(orbit.distance * fallCamData.zoom)). This zoom multiplier is less than 1.0, so the camera gets closer each frame. No code ever restores the distance back to normal after the fall ends.

After falling once, the camera stays permanently zoomed in. After falling multiple times, the camera becomes unusably close to the player.

Fix: Store the pre-fall camera distance and lerp back to it when the fall ends.

title: Wind Wraith not climbable during swoop - contradicts GDD
description: GDD states players must 'jump onto it from elevated platforms when it swoops'. However, in colossus/wraith.js isWraithClimbable (line ~434), climbing is only allowed during CIRCLING and STUNNED states. The SWOOPING state makes the wraith unclimbable, which is the exact opposite of the GDD intent. The swoop should be the primary climbing opportunity.

title: Add ground collision detection for physics-based movement
description: The physics world has gravity and ground colliders should exist once ejh is fixed. Need to detect when player is grounded via collision events and use that instead of manual ground height checks. Use adapter.onCollision() to register collision events between player body and ground bodies, check contact normal for downward-facing contacts to determine isGrounded.

title: Wind gusts start and end abruptly - no smooth transition
description: In world/wind.js, gust strength ramps up instantly (line ~36) and drops to 0 immediately when duration expires (line ~45). This causes sudden jarring forces on cape, particles, and potentially the player. Should fade in and out smoothly.

title: Wind Wraith swoop targets stale player position
description: In colossus/wraith.js line ~327, swoopTarget is set once when entering SWOOPING state. The wraith dives to where the player WAS, not where they ARE. By the time the swoop reaches the target position, the player has likely moved. This makes the wraith's swoop attack easy to dodge and looks unrealistic.

title: Physics step results never read back - player floats
description:
**Root cause:** After physicsAdapter.step() is called in main.js line 1097, the player position/velocity is NEVER read back via adapter.getPosition() or adapter.getVelocity(). The physics body acts as a write-only store.

Current broken flow:
1. Line 1094-1095: Player state pushed TO physics body
2. Line 1097: Physics step runs  
3. Line 1181: updatePlayer() called - uses adapter but doesn't read result
4. Lines 1226-1254: Manual collision overwrites state with raycast logic
5. Line 1260-1261: Player state pushed BACK to physics body (circular!)

Fix: After step(), read position and velocity back with getPosition/getVelocity. Use cannon-es collision events or world.contacts for isGrounded.

title: [bug] No combat feedback - no damage numbers, hit indicators, or visual feedback when attacking
description:
## Description
When the player attacks a colossus, there's no visual or audio feedback to indicate hits, misses, or damage dealt.

Expected feedback that is missing:
- Damage numbers floating up
- Screen shake on weak point hits
- Sound effects
- Colossus reaction animation

## Notes
Players attack blindly with no confirmation they hit anything.

title: All colossi floating above arena islands instead of standing on them
description:
In the debug page (http://localhost:3000/debug), all three colossi (Sentinel, Titan, Wraith) are floating above the ground instead of standing on their respective arena islands.

Expected behavior: Colossi should stand on their arena island surfaces.

The colossi positions are set at y=0 in main.js:420, but their AI state keeps them at elevated Y positions. The getGroundHeight() function exists but isn't being used to position colossi on the ground.

Related to: polyshadow-dtp (collision detection) - fixing collision would also address colossi positioning.

title: Particle system uses wall-clock time and frame-rate-dependent physics
description: world/particles.js: 1) performance.now() used instead of game time - breaks on pause/tab-switch. 2) Velocity integration frame-rate dependent (implicit *60 assumes 60fps). 3) Respawned particles inherit random lifetime as maxLifetime, breaking fade-out.

title: HUD not rendering: engine/hud.js never imported or called in main.js
description:
The entire HUD system is disconnected. engine/hud.js (createHUD) is never imported in game/main.js. The hud-canvas element exists in index.html but no code ever draws on it. This means:

- No stamina bar/arc is visible to the player
- No colossus health bar is shown
- No direction indicators are rendered
- The hud-canvas is stuck at default 300x150 size (never resized)

engine/ui.js has all the data methods (getStaminaArc, getColossusHealthVisual, getDirectionHint, getHUDState) fully implemented and tested, but they are never called from main.js.

Steps to fix:
1. Import createHUD in game/main.js
2. Initialize HUD with hud-canvas element, resize it to viewport
3. Call ui.getStaminaArc/getColossusHealthVisual/getDirectionHint each frame
4. Call hud.draw() each frame with computed state

title: Camera collision avoidance not implemented
description: In engine/camera.js, collisionLayers is stored but never used. The camera clips through all geometry (islands, colossi). The GDD specifies 'Collision avoidance (doesn't clip through geometry)' and 'Auto-adjusts distance based on context (close for climbing, far for exploration)'. Neither is implemented. The camera also has no snapToTarget method, causing a lerp glitch during arena teleportation (camera starts from old position).

title: Ending sequence is 18 seconds, completely unskippable
description: In game/ending-sequence.js, the ending takes 18 seconds with no skip mechanism. Players must wait through the full sequence every time. After seeing it once, this becomes frustrating. Add a 'skip' option (press any key / click to skip to credits).

title: Roll/Dodge mechanic from GDD is completely unimplemented
description:
The GDD specifies a 'Roll/Dodge (i-frames, stamina cost)' as part of the player moveset. No roll, dodge, or i-frame mechanic exists anywhere in the codebase. The player has no defensive option against colossus attacks beyond running away.

This is a core gameplay feature missing entirely.

title: Wire island meshes as cannon-es static trimesh colliders
description:
When island meshes are created in `game/main.js`, also create static trimesh colliders in the physics world from the island geometry.

**Steps:**
1. After `createIslandMesh`, extract the indexed BufferGeometry vertex/index data
2. Call `adapter.createTrimeshCollider(vertices, indices)` with the correct scale/position
3. Add all 4 island colliders (hub + 3 arena) to the physics world at startup
4. Remove the manual `getGroundHeight` function — cannon-es handles ground contact via collision events

**The player body should now collide with island surfaces naturally, with `isGrounded` derived from contact normals.**

**TDD:** Test that trimesh collider data is correctly extracted from island geometry (vertex/index format). Test that ground height via physics raycast matches getIslandSurfaceHeight.

**Files:** game/main.js, engine/renderer.js
